phrases.intent_en: Where is the flower market section?
phrases.cultural_note: Flower sections in Czech market halls are often near a side entrance for easy loading and fresh air.

likely_response.local: Pravým vchodem a pak hned doprava.
likely_response.translation_en: Through the right entrance and then immediately right.

responses.english: Through the right entrance and then immediately right.
responses.local: Pravým vchodem a pak hned doprava.

phrases.intent_en: Where are the local cheese vendors?
phrases.cultural_note: Many Czech markets group specialty vendors together; cheese is often near other fresh goods.

likely_response.local: V levé hale, vzadu u pekaře.
likely_response.translation_en: In the left hall, at the back near the baker.

responses.english: In the left hall, at the back near the baker.
responses.local: V levé hale, vzadu u pekaře.

phrases.intent_en: Do I weigh produce before the stall?
phrases.text_local: Mám si ovoce a zeleninu zvážit sám, než přijdu k pultu?
phrases.cultural_note: At many Czech markets, customers use a communal scale first so the vendor can price items quickly.

likely_response.local: Ano, váha je hned vedle stánku.
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the scale is right next to the stall.

responses.english: Yes, the scale is right next to the stall.
responses.local: Ano, váha je hned vedle stánku.

phrases.text_local: Dá se u vás platit kartou?
phrases.cultural_note: Small market stalls in the Czech Republic often operate cash-only, while permanent shops accept cards.
